title: From cloning to gene-editing: the enduring legacy of Dolly the sheep
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02096-1

Dolly’s brush with celebrity could hold lessons for current discussions about reproductive technologies.

title: Universities are relying on AI-detection software to catch cheating. How well do the programs work?
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01358-2

Technology companies say that their services can sniff out AI-generated writing, but their tools vary in technique and quality.

title: Deep-sea oddities and boatloads of other new species — June’s best science images
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02087-2

The month’s sharpest science shots — selected by Nature’s photo team.

title: The rise of computer chips — and the race to control them
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02099-y

An analysis sets out the history of computing beyond software and Californian entrepreneurs.

title: Time to give hydration breaks the red card? What science says about keeping cool
content:
Nature, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02097-0

Inappropriate use of cooling breaks in sport is undermining trust in heat-health research.

title: Daily briefing: How cooperation built the world
content:
Nature, Published online: 03 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02116-0

Collaboration in nature has often been overlooked in favour of competition, argues science journalist Rowan Hooper. Plus, what’s really happening with trust in science.

title: Temporal potential-induced regulation for extending alkaline electrolyzer lifespan under power fluctuations
content: Xia et al. report the temporal potential-induced regulation for large-scale alkaline water electrolyzers. Without modifying the catalyst and electrolyzer structures, this approach extends AWE durability via power supply optimization, providing a scalable and effective pathway. This work bridges electrical engineering and electrochemistry, advancing the integration of renewable energy into hydrogen infrastructure.

title: Electrical double-layer dynamics regulation for maximized electrostatic energy harvesting of liquid-solid triboelectric nanogenerators
content: Water-solid friction produces clean and durable electricity upon environmental excitation. By revealing and overcoming the fundamental limitations of liquid-solid triboelectricity, a switch-enhanced generator achieves high energy output, good load adaptability, and can harvest energy from multiple scenarios to power electronics.

title: Engineered living materials need engineered EU regulation
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02651-1

Kai Purnhagen and Justus Wesseler discuss how EU law needs reform to address market access bottlenecks for living composites.

title: Twist-angle-controlled anomalous gating in bilayer graphene/BN heterostructures
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02667-7

Rotational misalignment between the top and bottom boron nitride layers encapsulating graphene acts as a switch for anomalous gating effects, such as gate ineffectiveness and hysteresis. These effects are observed at room temperature within an angle range of 15–45° and are highly sensitive to small angular changes.

title: Multimodal scanning-probe quantum sensing of quantum materials
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02648-w

Solid-state spin defects are a powerful and versatile class of quantum sensor. This Review covers the development of scanning-probe nitrogen-vacancy sensing as a tool for investigating quantum materials with nanoscale resolution, surveys the recent development of spin defects in 1D and 2D host materials and identifies key directions for future research.

title: Thermally activated proton transfer enables air-processed perovskite solar cells approaching 27%
content: Making high-performance perovskite solar cells in ordinary air is difficult because moisture and heat disrupt film formation. Here, a small molecular additive guides crystal growth at the start and then stabilizes the material during annealing, enabling highly efficient devices made outside a glovebox. The work shows that additive design can control both processing and stability at once, offering a practical path toward lower-cost, scalable perovskite solar manufacturing.

title: In situ optical techniques for perovskite crystallization
content: In situ optical characterization techniques have emerged as powerful tools for probing nucleation, phase evolution, and intermediate-state formation in real time. This review provides a systematic overview of the development and application of in situ optical techniques for studying perovskite crystallization. It aims to offer a comprehensive technical guide for researchers seeking to employ in situ optical methods to investigate perovskite crystallization.

title: Switching from insertion to conversion for multielectron aqueous vanadium batteries
content:
Nature Materials, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02666-8

Conventional aqueous vanadium-ion batteries have low capacities, as the fundamental charge-storage mechanism is a one-electron insertion process. Here, using a mesoporous carbon shell approach, a four-electron conversion reaction for charge storage is demonstrated.
